document_b.label: Document B
document_b.document_type: Invoice PDF with multiple embedded invoice documents / scanned image-based

activated_rules.rule_name: PDF with multiple documents; pairing required before verification
Consignor may be verified if explicitly found anywhere in invoice files
At least one quantity may be verified unless explicit contradiction exists
Each origin in certificate must be explicitly identifiable in invoice

consignor.certificate_value:
SKF Sverige AB
Kvibergs Broväg 8
SE-415 05, GÖTEBORG
Sweden
consignor.invoice_value:
Issued by:
SKF Sverige AB
von Utfallsgatan 4
415 50 Göteborg
Sweden
consignor.motivation: Certificate consignor name 'SKF Sverige AB' is explicitly identifiable in the invoice material, including page 1 footer and repeated footer blocks across the scanned invoice pages. Country 'Sweden' also matches. Address differences do not in themselves cause mismatch.

consignee.certificate_value:
SKF Vietnam CO Ltd
105 Ton Dat Tien,Tan Phu Ward
700000 Ho Chi Minh
Viet Nam
consignee.invoice_value: Page 1 of selected invoice document shows 'Ship to' with country 'Türkiye' and no explicit occurrence of 'SKF Vietnam CO Ltd' or Vietnam in the visible selected invoice pages.
consignee.motivation: The certificate consignee is 'SKF Vietnam CO Ltd' in Viet Nam. The best-paired invoice document visible in pages 1-6 is addressed to Türkiye and does not explicitly identify the certificate consignee in buyer/ship-to/delivery/other recipient fields. This is a direct contradiction, not merely additional information.

goods_description.invoice_value: Selected invoice document pages 1-6 list goods such as DEEP GROOVE BALL BRG OD > 30 MM, CARB BEARING, SPHERICAL ROLLER BRG.
goods_description.motivation: The certificate goods are 'LOCK WASHER' and 'NUTS'. The invoice document paired by consignor and document structure on pages 1-6 does not contain these goods; instead it contains bearing products. The certificate invoice/reference number also does not point to that invoice. Therefore the goods description cannot be verified and is contradicted by the selected invoice.

quantity.invoice_value: Selected invoice document pages 1-6 show quantities such as 10, 5, 5, 67 and total 87; no explicit 2 pcs / 2 pcs for the certificate goods in the selected paired invoice.
quantity.motivation: The certificate contains explicit quantities with unit ('2 pcs' and '2 pcs'). In the selected invoice document, no matching explicit quantity for the certificate goods can be identified; the visible invoice quantities concern different goods and materially different totals. Quantity therefore cannot be verified and is contradicted by the selected invoice.

origin.motivation: The certificate origin is 'SWEDEN'. The selected invoice document explicitly states 'Country of origin' and 'Sweden' on page 1, so the origin can be positively verified.

critical_failures: Consignee mismatch: certificate shows SKF Vietnam CO Ltd / Viet Nam while selected invoice shows Türkiye and no explicit consignee match.
Goods description mismatch: certificate lists LOCK WASHER and NUTS while selected invoice pages 1-6 list bearing products.
Quantity mismatch: certificate shows 2 pcs and 2 pcs, not identifiable in selected invoice.
Document set contains multiple invoice documents within one scanned PDF; pairing was required and the available material indicates inconsistency with the certificate.
overall_assessment.decision_summary: The certificate can be classified with high confidence. The invoice PDF contains multiple embedded invoice documents. Applying the pairing rule conservatively, the visible invoice document most clearly functioning as the primary invoice (pages 1-6) matches the consignor and origin but contradicts the certificate on consignee, goods, and quantity. Therefore the document pair is NOT_IDENTICAL and requires manual handling.
